props.name: addEntry
props.description: append an entry. Same Location across Collections = multiple entries.

props.name: create
props.description: start an empty Collection.

props.name: export
props.description: emit the Collection as a portable bundle for backup or sharing.

props.name: import
props.description: ingest an external collection (KML, GeoJSON, RTP bundle, third-party share URL). Becomes a first-class Collection owned by the user.

props.name: removeEntry
props.description: drop an entry from this Collection. Does not delete the Location or its Reviews in other Collections.

props.name: share
props.description: produce a shareable artifact (deferred to a later sharing concept).

props.name: addToCollection
props.description: make this Location an entry in a Collection (this is `Collection.addEntry` viewed from the Location's side; see synchronization below).

props.name: dropPin
props.description: create a `manual` Location with a generated UUID.

props.name: import
props.description: adopt a Location from a KML placemark, GeoJSON feature, or shared URL.

props.name: merge
props.description: user-confirmed reconciliation when two Locations from different providers refer to the same real place.

props.name: openExternally
props.description: hand off to an external map app (e.g. Google Maps, Apple Maps) for navigation, street view, or richer details. RTP does not reimplement those affordances; it cedes them by handoff.

props.name: refresh
props.description: if `refreshable`, re-query the provider to update `cachedMetadata`.

props.name: resolve
props.description: search a provider for a place; return a candidate Location.

props.name: jumpToCollection
props.description: fit viewport to that Collection's entries.

props.name: open
props.description: launch action. Restore `viewport`, render `visiblePins` for all Collections in `collectionFilter`. Renders before any background data has loaded (see doc01.01 §"Cold-start playbook").

props.name: pan
props.description: standard map navigation. Updates `viewport`.

props.name: selectPin
props.description: focus an entry; reveal its Collection and per-entry data.

props.name: toggleCollection
props.description: add/remove a Collection from `collectionFilter`. Persistent.

props.name: zoom
props.description: standard map navigation. Updates `viewport`.

props.name: clear
props.description: unset a field where "not set" is meaningful (distinct from "false" for booleans, etc.).

props.name: defineTemplate
props.description: initial template at Collection creation.

props.name: edit
props.description: change values within the template.

props.name: editTemplate
props.description: modify. Existing instances reconcile (missing required fields prompt; removed fields archived but not destroyed).

props.name: start
props.description: produce a draft Review for a Location in this Collection. Pre-filled with sensible defaults (today's date, Unvisited, etc.).

props.name: submit
props.description: commit the instance. Becomes the entry's Review.

props.name: useBuiltIn
props.description: adopt a built-in template (Coffee Ranking, Wishlist, Geo Diary, etc.) as the starting point.
One Collection: its entries shown as either a map or a list projection (toggleable). Entry point for adding/removing entries, editing the template, and exporting/sharing.
Author or edit a Review instance against the Collection's template. Two substates: `editing` (the form is open) and `submitting` (commit in flight).

props.name: Collection
props.purpose: Let a user organize a set of Location entries under one shared review template, so they can build a personal record of any kind of place-bound activity (cafés ranked, restaurants visited, days journaled).
props.state:
- `name` — user-provided label.
- `entries` — set of `(Location, Review)` pairs. The same Location may appear in many Collections; each appearance is a distinct entry with its own Review.
- `created`, `last_modified` — for sorting and sync.
- `appearance` — visual identity used on the map (color, pin style). Set on creation; editable.

The Collection's review template lives in the Review concept (§3), not here. The Collection knows that *its* entries' Reviews share a template, but does not own the template's structure.
props.operationalPrinciple: A user creates a Collection "Drip Coffee," authors its Review template (via §3) to include stars and coffee-style, and `addEntry(BlueBottleMintPlaza, review)` where the Review carries the per-place data. Later they add Blue Bottle to a different Collection "Geo Diary" with a different Review template; the two entries are independent. Browsing Drip Coffee shows only the coffee-shaped entries.

props.name: Location
props.purpose: Let a user designate a real-world place once, and refer to it from anywhere in the app, regardless of which provider supplied its identity.
props.state:
- `coordinates` — `(lat, lng)`. Always present.
- `displayName` — human-readable label.
- `sourceType` — which provider asserted this Location: `google`, `osm`, `apple`, `manual`, etc.
- `sourceId` — stable id within that provider (`place_id`, OSM node id, generated UUID for `manual`).
- `cachedMetadata` — provider-supplied snapshot at import time (address, category, etc.).
- `refreshable` — whether the source can be re-queried for updated metadata.

`(sourceType, sourceId)` is the identity. Two Locations with the same `(lat, lng)` from different providers are distinct.
props.operationalPrinciple: A user searches "Blue Bottle Mint Plaza" via the Google provider and resolves a Location. They add it to two Collections. Later they tap the Location and choose "Open in Google Maps"; the OS hands off to the Google Maps app, which opens at the same coordinates. Their entries in both Collections are unaffected.

props.name: Map Overview
props.purpose: Give the user, on every app launch, an immediate spatial view of everything they care about — all Collections, all entries, with enough visual encoding to read the state of their world at a glance.
props.state:
- `viewport` — current `(center, zoom, bearing)`. Persisted across launches.
- `visiblePins` — for each entry across all Collections, a pin styled by:
  - **fill color** = the owning Collection's `appearance` color
  - **outline** = same color as fill for visited entries; pin is **gray with a colored outline** for unvisited entries (where the schema has a Visited/Unvisited concept)
- `collectionFilter` — set of Collections currently shown. Defaults to "all."
- `selectedPin` — currently focused entry, if any.
props.operationalPrinciple: A user taps the RTP icon. Within ~200ms, they see their last viewport with pins from all their Collections: orange-filled pins for "Drip Coffee" (visited), orange-outlined gray pins for cafés on their wishlist, green-filled pins for "NYT Top 100" places they've been, green-outlined gray pins for the rest of the NYT list. Without any further action, they understand the state of their world. Tapping a pin reveals which Collection it belongs to and the entry's data.

props.name: Review
props.purpose:
Let a user capture a structured evaluation of a Location within the context of a particular Collection — restaurants reviewed by stars and dish notes; coffee shops by roast style and a power-ranking position; diary entries by date and freeform text.

A Review has two faces, kept inside one concept because they share a single purpose (defining and capturing the evaluation):

1. A **template** — the shape of the form, authored once per Collection.
2. An **instance** — the filled-in data for a specific entry.
props.state:
Per Collection (template):

- `template` — an ordered list of fields. Each field has a `name`, a `type` (e.g. `stars`, `text`, `enum`, `boolean`, `date`, `power-ranking`), per-type configuration (e.g. `stars: 1–5`; `enum: [light, medium, dark]`), and a `required` flag.
- `template_version` — bumped when the template changes; used to reconcile instances.

Per entry (instance):

- `data` — a map of `field-name → value`, conforming to the template at the recorded version.
- `recorded_template_version` — the template version this instance was written against.
- `created`, `last_modified`.
props.operationalPrinciple: A user creating "Drip Coffee" authors the template: a 1–5 stars field, a `light|medium|dark` style enum, a Visited boolean, a power-ranking field, a freeform notes field. Later, sitting at a café, they `start(DripCoffee, BlueBottleMintPlaza)`. The app shows the template's form; they fill in stars=4, style=light, visited=true, notes="excellent", and `submit`. The entry now carries this Review. Browsing the Collection, they see entries shaped exactly by their template.

props.description: The primary screen the user is on. Persistent — does not change when overlays open and close.

What sheet/modal is currently overlaid on top of the nav surface. Transitions to non-`none` open an overlay; transitions back to `none` close it. Multi-target transitions can simultaneously close an overlay and change the underlying nav state (see ReviewForm.submitting.SUBMITTED).
Entries shown as a scrollable list, sortable by date, distance, or power-ranking.
Entries shown as pins on a map fitted to the Collection's bounds.
Manage the user's Collections. Create new ones, open existing ones, import external lists.
One entry — the (Location, Review) pair. Read-mostly surface; mutation goes through the ReviewForm overlay. Hosts the 'open externally' handoff to Google Maps / Apple Maps / etc.
The everything view. Default landing surface. Shows pins for every entry in every visible Collection, colored by Collection appearance and styled by visited/unvisited state. See doc01.03 §4.
Configure providers, BYOK keys, sync target. Surface is intentionally under-specified until the relevant concept actions exist.
Location-first flow: a Location is already chosen (dropped pin, shared link, etc.). User picks which Collection to add it to.
Import an external collection. Stub — concrete steps (source picker, schema mapping, preview, confirm) will be detailed when the import surface is unfolded.
Resolve, drop, or import a Location. Search via a configured provider, drop a manual pin, or import from a URL / file (KML, share link).
Rest state — no overlay. The user sees only the underlying nav surface.
User is filling in template fields.
Submit in flight. Brief transient state; transitions both close the overlay and route nav to EntryDetail of the just-saved entry.

Author or edit a Collection's Review template — the schema that governs every Review in this Collection. Add/remove/reorder fields; pick field types (stars, enum, boolean, date, power-ranking, text). Adopt a built-in template as a starting point.
Return to the everything view.
User starts the Collection-first add flow. LocationPicker resolves a Location, then ReviewForm captures the per-entry Review.
User opens the Review template editor for this Collection.
User opens a single entry to inspect its Location and Review.
User exports this Collection as a portable bundle (KML / GeoJSON / RTP bundle).
User produces a shareable artifact. Sharing concept deferred — see doc01.04.
User switches to the map view of this Collection.
User switches to the list view of this Collection.
Return to the everything view.
User opens a Collection to see its entries.
User imports an external collection (KML, GeoJSON, RTP bundle, third-party share URL).
User starts a new Collection. Opens the SchemaBuilder overlay to author the Review template before any entries can be added.
Return to the Collection.
User edits the Review for this entry.
User hands off to an external map app (Google Maps, Apple Maps, OsmAnd, ...) for navigation or street view. RTP does not track what happens after.
If the Location is refreshable, re-query the provider for updated metadata.
User removes this entry from the Collection. Does not delete the Location — it may exist in other Collections.
User drops a pin at their current location (or a tapped map point) and chooses which Collection to add it to. Starts the Location-first flow from doc01.02 §3.
User toggles a Collection's visibility from the legend. Stays on this surface; the pin set updates.
User opens the list of Collections to manage them.
User picks a Collection from the legend to fit the viewport to its entries.
User taps a pin on the map to inspect that entry.
User opens settings (providers, BYOK keys, sync target).
Return to the everything view.
Cancel; close the overlay. Underlying nav remains where it was.
User picks a Collection; advance to fill out the Review.
None of the existing Collections fit; create a new one.
Cancel the import.
Import committed; close the overlay. Underlying nav (CollectionList) refreshes.
Cancel the add flow; close the overlay.
User long-presses the map to drop a manual pin.
User pastes a share URL or selects a KML/GeoJSON file.
User picks one candidate; advance to fill out the Review.
User types a search query; provider returns candidates.
User explicitly unsets a field. Distinct from 'false' for booleans (three-state widget).
User changes the value of any field.
User cancels without saving. Draft is discarded, overlay closes.
User submits the Review.
Submit succeeded; close the overlay. (Underlying nav routing to EntryDetail will be handled by an action once the visualizer tolerates multi-target transitions; see doc02.02.01.)
Cancel without committing template changes.
Initial template authoring at Collection creation.
Template authored or edited; close the overlay. Caller's underlying nav resumes — typically CollectionDetail.
Modify an existing template. Existing Reviews reconcile against the new shape.
Adopt a built-in template (Coffee Ranking, Wishlist, Geo Diary) as the starting point.
